deductions.description: Slight arch in pre-flight, not a tight hollow
Flexed feet/loss of toe point in pre-flight
Slight pike at hips on table contact
Hips pass vertical before hands fully leave table (late block)
Flexed feet/loss of toe point in post-flight

deduction_log.reason: Multiple form breaks in pre-flight, block, and post-flight, leading to a forward-leaning landing with a step.

special_requirements.comment: Gymnast performed a Handspring Vault, which is a B-value element.

artistry.notes: General lack of consistent body tension and pointed toes throughout the vault sequence contributes to a minor artistry deduction.

coaching_summary: The gymnast executed a Handspring Vault with good aggression in the run-up and sufficient height in the post-flight. However, there were consistent form breaks in body shape (slight arch/pike), knee tension, and toe point throughout the pre-flight, block, and post-flight phases. The block off the table was slightly late with bent arms, leading to a forward-leaning landing that required a deep squat and a step to control. Focus on refining fundamental body shapes and active blocking mechanics will significantly improve future vault performances.
top_3_fixes: Develop a tight hollow body position from the springboard contact through the pre-flight, maintaining locked knees and pointed toes to maximize block efficiency.
Execute a powerful, active block off the table with fully locked arms and an aggressive shoulder push, ensuring hips do not pass vertical before hands leave the table.
Improve landing control by focusing on a higher chest position and absorbing impact through the legs with a controlled, shallow knee bend, aiming to stick the landing without steps or hops.
celebrations: Good aggression and speed in the run-up to the vault.
Successfully completed the Handspring Vault without a fall.
Achieved good height in the post-flight phase of the vault.
